frameworks.name: continual learning
frameworks.one_line: sequential acquisition of multiple tasks without mutual interference.

frameworks.name: factorised task representations
frameworks.one_line: task structure encoded as separable factors that resist interference.

frameworks.name: neural networks / machine learning
frameworks.one_line: current ML systems fail at continual learning, unlike humans.

core_question: What cognitive mechanisms allow humans to learn multiple tasks in succession without catastrophic interference?
key_assumptions: temporal autocorrelation of training objectives can shape representational structure
individuals vary in prior tendency to factorise task space
error patterns index underlying task representation
manipulations: training schedule: temporally autocorrelated (focussed) vs randomly interleaved
individual differences in baseline factorisation tendency
key_findings: focussed training improved learning of new tasks compared to interleaved
focussed-trained participants performed better on later randomly interleaved test
error patterns implied focussed training fostered factorised task representations
participants with stronger prior factorisation tendency gained more from focussed training
interpretations: focussed learning enables factorised representations protected from mutual interference
building artificial agents that factorise tasks could solve continual learning in ML
